Title: Natural variation of MS2 confers male fertility and drives hybrid breeding in soybean

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: required for male fertility in related systems, but not established here for soybean GmAMS2
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRISPR knockout showed no visible effect on male fertility and plants could produce seeds
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: U3 and U6 promoters for CRISPR guide expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Close paralog of MS2/GmAMS1 with 78.3% amino acid identity; likely evolved divergent functions; not a core causal gene for the fertility/metabolism phenotype in this study but experimentally tested
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited value for inducing male sterility alone based on current evidence
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmAMS2 is a close paralog of MS2/GmAMS1 and is preferentially expressed in anther. 2. Two independent CRISPR knockout mutants retained male fertility. 3. GmAMS2 therefore is not required for male fertility under the tested conditions and may have diverged functionally.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: sporopollenin/pollen wall building blocks (fatty acids and derivatives for microspore wall)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: required for microspore cell wall formation and male fertility
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pollen wall formation and male fertility; loss of function causes complete male sterility with failure to produce pollen grains and reduced wax/cutin deposition in anthers
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of secondary metabolites, lipid metabolism, pollen wall precursor formation, callose/primexine/intine-related wall formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via direct binding to G-box motifs in target promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positively regulates genes involved in secondary metabolite biosynthesis and lipid metabolism; activates direct targets among the 279 downregulated genes in ms2 mutant
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CYP98A2; ABCG21; AHL13; PGDH1; HMG1; LACS9; DTX35; PAL1; COI1; GSL05; GSL07; PGF1; AGP18; GATL1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: central regulator of soybean anther development and pollen wall formation; essential activator for male fertility
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: anther development from early flower bud to before pollination; field outcrossing evaluation with honeybees; long-day growth conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: predominantly expressed in flower; exclusively expressed in stamen (anther and filament); highest at early stamen development and decreases during flower development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: predominantly expressed in flower; exclusively expressed in stamen (anther and filament); highest at early stamen development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: map-based cloning; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; Western blot; RNA-seq; ChIP-seq; EMSA; transient dual-LUC assay; cytological analysis; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GmU6 promoter for CRISPR guide expression; 35S mini promoter in reporter; 35S promoter in effector constructs
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA-seq; RNA-seq; ChIP-seq; NCBI SRA: PRJNA900526
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious differences in vegetative growth characters reported; mutation specifically affects male fertility, causing undeveloped tiny pods on sterile plants
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also named GmAMS1; homolog of Arabidopsis AMS. MS2 can directly bind G-box in vitro and activate reporter expression, whereas mutant ms2 protein lacks function. Study emphasizes breeding value for hybrid soybean due to high outcrossing rate and minimal pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent target to generate stable male-sterile lines for hybrid soybean breeding; ms2/crms2 showed best outcrossing among tested male sterile mutants and is suitable for biotechnology-based male sterility systems
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MS2 was cloned as Glyma.10G281800, a bHLH transcription factor responsible for the classic ms2 male-sterile phenotype in soybean. 2. CRISPR knockout of MS2 reproduced complete male sterility, confirming causality. 3. MS2 is specifically expressed in early stamen development, localizes to the nucleus, binds G-box motifs, and acts as a transcriptional activator. 4. Integrated RNA-seq and ChIP-seq showed MS2 directly activates genes involved in lipid metabolism and secondary metabolite biosynthesis required for pollen wall formation. 5. Loss of MS2 blocks microsporogenesis at tetrad stage and provides a valuable male-sterile tool for hybrid breeding.
